item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Fitting music.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This image is pure.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Due to the original image not being CC Compliant, it has been replaced with permission from the author.

Image Source: CC BY-SA 2.0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My in-depth analysis about why I think this page is a worthy entry starts with how you characterized the

You basically had me a small bird shouting "stranglefruit" as loud as thunderclaps. Nice to see something simple and funny like this! I didn't see the crosstests but I bet this article is better on it's own. I also really like the idea another poster had - realistically, most birds would be at least this annoying if they could talk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoa. How did I not realize that? You're absolutely right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was actually in the original version of the skip but the author removed it because people didn't like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really feel like this can be further enhanced by giving us some of the names of the other SCPs that wish to talk to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just…I don’t know. I just don’t see any appeal really to it. It’s a bird that just babbles and may or may not be well known to humanoid anomalies. I feel like I’m missing a lot more than I’m supposed to be.

EDIT: I read the short companion story and that does radically change my opinion, but I feel like that information should be hinted at in the actual article (unless I’m just dense and missed it) somehow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. ██████: Please repeat that statement in the dialect used by the Foundation.

SCP-2337: Ten-four, ex-lax!

SCP-2337 begins yodeling for three consecutive minutes.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every time I read the interview, I get giddily happy. God I love this weird-ass birb
Congratulations, Dr Spanko, you earned it.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed making this a little too much; I hope ya'll enjoy hearing it. And now that I've seen the above comment about "A Diminished Thing," I need to record the tragedy to go along with it. Stay tuned.

https://youtu.be/t5WyWzeOvxU

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is amazing, but with Peperghost’s new article; I think this might be one of my favourites

Check the out- it puts a whole new spin on it

http://www.scp-wiki.net/a-diminished-thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: for my favorite bird...

item.title: Re: Excardon Me...
item.content:encoded: True… both are equally as incomprehensible.

item.title: Re: Excardon Me...
item.content:encoded: Alternatively, Homsar.

item.title: Excardon Me...
item.content:encoded: I just now realized… this is basically Senor Cardgage in SCP form! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, this is one of the more realistic SCP'S I know about. This is what a talking bird would be like. That's why I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, but we don't have a similar expression based on numbers, so I just kept it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Curious, is "dix-quatre" an actual expression in French like ten-four is in English?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"And how!" is a literal translation. Too perfect. X3

I read just enough French to know Dave should be very, very happy.
This nails the translation from Spankolish to Spankçais.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It actually is! It means something like "indeed!", but it's stronger than that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Et comment!

(Is that a phrase in French?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Herr Doktor Spanko recently became unexpectedly popular on the French branch chat, but it was also deemed untranslatable. Challenge accepted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to SCP-2337's poor grasp of cause and effect, punishments and rewards will inevitably result in frustration on part of involved personnel.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, definitely one of the better newer SCPs (to many newer ones try to hard) though I'm apparently in the minority of people who actually liked the crosstesting, personally I'd suggest making a crosstesting log separate from the main article and include a larger and more diverse set of crosstests.

item.title: Re: CACK!
item.content:encoded: So, wait at least a year?

item.title: Re: CACK!
item.content:encoded: Heritage says articles must be 3 years old to get onto there and this is really recent, also its probably not going to get updated anytime soon.

item.title: CACK!
item.content:encoded: In my opinion, Spanko should be added to the Heritage Collection; it's that good.

Spanko is totally 1337

or should I say 2337

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just for posterity/those late to the party, there's not really an in-joke so much as that Dr. Spanko has become an in-joke: The running thing is that he may or may not be any or all of the following:

  • a cosmic (possibly divine) being on extended vacation in the form of a corncrake
  • a looney toons character set loose in the SCPverse
  • a planeswalker with vast knowledge of the multiverse
  • an unfathomably ancient immortal
  • a being from a neighboring plane of existence where he makes total sense
  • any of the above, but also mildly insane and ditzy
  • all of the above

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upcackingvoted.

If there's an in-joke here, I don't get it, but I don't even care. As a much earlier poster said: This is the right kind of stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have found my new favorite SCP of all time CACK.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the best thing. I wish I was in on the obviously extant and eminently excellent in-joke that is Dr. Spanko.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tried to make it +101 but realized I already rated this.
Oh well, still gives me a laugh whenever I read it. We need more Spanko.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stranglefruits for all!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey. My ridiculous screaming bird son is my first mainlister to hit +100. Not bad, Spanko.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I'm reminded of Sholto Unwerth, from the WH40k Ravenor series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As the author, I'm just going to put it here since I can't really fit it into the article:

Dr. Spanko is a HUGE fan of Tom Jones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the author, I am genuinely flattered xD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the dumbest shit I've ever read and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Snackutations Doctor Spanko, cack! You am authoritater? Explainerate.

Honestly, if someone insulted my mother like that, I'd also want to peck out his eyes.

+1 for making such a deep character. Dr. Spanko has so much depth and character development, and personally would make an interesting in-depth analysis to a protagonist's overbearing story arch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should probably clarify - Dr. blank was sitting at an interview table and Spanko was standing on the table.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Asides from this being superficially hillarious, think of what the researchers must be going through.
"I spent half my life studying biology to end up taking care of a living internet meme that subsists on gummy worms. And I can't even post it on imgur. Where did it all go wrong?"

Seconding this in particular. I got the same impression, and it has a lot to do with why I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The edits to this make it work for me. I would've been fine with keeping (but expanding) the crosslinks, but I don't miss them; and oddly enough, "three minutes of consecutive yodeling" does seem less of a joke than "five minutes of consecutive yodeling", and the "Please discontinue yodeling" addition immediately makes me feel sorry for the doctor (who's clearly just sat there trying to figure out what the fuck to even say here for three minutes) and therefore makes me willing to believe the conversation. The rest of the edits are good, too.

Changed vote to +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a bit confused by the fact that it manages to peck at the Dr's face. Shouldn't it be pecking at his ankles? That would probably be funnier whilst simultaneously less -J like

I'm also not sure about this "SCP-2337 begins yodeling for three consecutive minutes." That's an unreasonably long time, and the Dr. wouldn't just sit there for minutes before asking him to stop.

item.title: I dun goofed?
item.content:encoded:
I was trying to see the previous versions and I think I may have changed everything? Oops?

Edit: I think i fixed it. Again, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is hilarious and also kind of terrifying +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thing reminds me of some of the more annoying characters from the Redwall series. Which isn't a bad thing, I very much enjoyed it. I do like some of the suggestions from Smapti and sirpudding though, and wouldn't mind seeing them implemented into the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It me! Cack!

Asides from this being superficially hillarious, think of what the researchers must be going through.
"I spent half my life studying biology to end up taking care of a living internet meme that subsists on gummy worms. And I can't even post it on imgur. Where did it all go wrong?"

Also, why the volume redaction? Anything above 194dB counts as a shockwave rather than a sound.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It done beforth hencewise! CACK!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this a reference to Jeph Jacques' Yelling Bird?
'Cause I can see a lot of similarities between the two…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WHAT SAY? AM PLEASE WITH DEVELOPMANTLE. PICTURE OF BEING IS COUSIN-MINE, THAT. OWES HIM ME SEVERAL STRANGLEFRUITAGE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Today's featured article on Wikipedia.

Cack.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoting, because its a mad duck that might or might not be mad, and/or a duck.

Or a goose. One of the two.

Anyway, it has a rare warmth and good-natured feel to it.

Would you call A Midsummer Night's Dream a joke book? No. Likewise I wouldn't put this in the J section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this version a lot. I kind of miss the way earlier version details about him being some kind of extradimensional dignitary/Foundation higher-up, but killing the cross-tests was the right choice. It might not be the best of all possible Dr. Spanko articles, but it is good. +1ing

Also, for reference, no, mainlisters don't have to be even remotely scary. They can be whatever you want! I just think people couldn't take the cross-tests seriously, which hurt immersion, but might fly in a -J.

Edit: You might want to add a footnote explaining that word salad is a technical term and define it, but I don't think anyone has complained, so it's probably not a big issue. Kudos for using psych terms, though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good points. Editing now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two things: first, species names have the first word capitalized, so it should be Crex crex. Second, shattering refers to hard/brittle objects. What happened to the agents' eardrums would likely be better described as "rupturing".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw this one after several edits, so I can't comment on its prior state, but I very much liked the version I saw, so I'm judging it on that. It's definitely not -J material in my opinion. It's weird, yes, but the Foundation is taking it seriously.
